PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Dec-07-2010 Continued To Mar-15-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Randall Schmidt, Esq. for 2006 Will Beneficiaries; Ciaran O'Sullivan, Esq. for Contestants Norma and Kum Hong; Bruce Feder, Esq. for Special Administrator Jeffrey Schornack; Nancy Levin, Esq. for SF General Hospital Foundation; California Pacific Med. Ctr.; Children's Hospital; St. Dominic's; Alex Aldrich in pro per.

Ms. Levin reported that counsel were in process of discovery, with goal of pro bono mediation sometime in February and would like to put out another conference status on mid-March. Mr. Feder addresses the court as to the status of the inventory on the appraisal of the real property and things that require an appraisal. The Court addressed counsel issues on filing of the inventory, special administrator, no general powers, creditor claims, Wells Fargo account and having a general administrator appointed. After further discussions on the record and with stipulation by counsel the petitions and will contest are continued to March 15, 2011 for status of mediation.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: S. Ravinantapricha. Bailiff: Deputy Santiago.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Aug-17-2010 Continued To Dec-07-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Bruce Feder for Jeffrey Schornack (Special Administrator). Charles Wolff for San Francisco General Hopsital Foundation, California Pacific Medical Center Foundation, Chlidren's Hospital Center for Research Foundation, Saint Dominic's Catholic Church (Residuary Beneficiaries of the 2008 Will). Randall Schmidt for Ten of the 2006 Will Beneficiaries. Ciarin O'Sullivan for Kum Hong and Norma Hong (. Alexander Aldrich in Propria Persona.

Mr. Wolff stated that he and Mr. O'Sullivan have begun discovery, that Mr. Schmidt planned to file some sort of contest, and that he and Mr. O'Sullivan would like to come back in 90 days. Mr. Schmidt stated that he has the contest and the petition for probate prepared but that he has not filed it because he is waiting for commitment from all Parties. Mr. Aldrich expressed his concerns with regard to Mr. Schornack being appointed as Administrator, spoke of the Decedent's wishes, stated that he filed a Creditor's Claim due to the Decedent giving away his Father's belongings and that he was seeking Counsel. The matter was continued to December 7, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for further status of discovery and trial setting. Mr. Schornack is to file an Inventory and Appraisal by stipulation of all Parties.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar May-04-2010 Continued To Aug-10-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Bruce Feder for and with Jeffrey Schornack. Ciarin O'Sullivan for Norma Hong and Kum Hong (Contestants). Charles Wolff for San Francisco Genreral Hospital Foundation, California Pacific Medical Center Foundation, St. Dominics Church and Childrens Hospital Oakland (Remainder Beneficiaries). Representatives for the Remainder Beneficiaries were also present. Emma Velasquez (Niece) and Alexander Aldrich (Godson) were also present.

The Parties agreed to extend the Letters until further order of the Court, to set a bond at $250,000.00 for the bank accounts and to have the $900,000.00 brokerage account placed into a blocked account. Mr. Feder stated that he would come back to Court and request an increase in bond or additional blocked accounts if other accounts were found and that he would share all information with all Parties as to what is found. Mr. Wolff stated that he was not in a position to evaluate mediation at this point. Ms. Valesquez stated that she was named in the first will, that she was not notified of the Court proceedings and requested time to hire an Attorney. Mr. Aldrich expressed concern about not receiving notice of the Decedent's death. The matter was continued to August 10, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Status of filing of the bond and the receipt for the blocked account was set for May 11, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Mar-30-2010 Continued To May-04-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Leslie Bishop for Children's Hospital. Bruce Feder for Jeffrey Schornack. Ciaran O'Sullivan for Norma Hong. Myrna Mariscal Cole in pro per. Alexander Aldredge in pro per. Myrna Parisse was also present. B. Franco (Interested Party) was also present.

Myrna Parisse stated that her Mother (Myrna Mariscal Cole) needed time to obtain an attorney. Mr. Feder stated that they were ready to proceed; however, he understood if the Court wished to continue the matter. The Court stated that it was inclined to continue the matter to give any individual time to obtain counsel. Mr. Feder stated that he would like to submit an ex-parte for appointment of a Special Administrator for the reasons set forth on the record. Mr. Franco stated that he was just here to follow the proceedings. The Court directed Mr. Feder to file the ex-parte. The Court continued the matter to May 4, 2010.

Judge: Mary Wiss. Court Reporter: Nici Rodich, CSR #11604. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.
